titleStmt.title: Letter from May Fay to James Finn, 21 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: May Fay

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from May Fay (1897-1976) to James Finn (1877-1922) informing him of local news. Throughout the letter May has hidden five kisses for James to find.May Fay became engaged to James Finn in January 1916. Despite the twenty year age gap their letters show a very personal and private insight into courting in early 20th century Ireland. Following her husband's death May held on to his letters as her reminder of him. May never remarried and focused her attention instead on her businesses in order to provide for their children.James Finn was a civil servant, working in the National Health Service Commission on Mount Street. While neither James nor May were politically active their letters provide a very unique insight into the affairs of Easter Week and its aftermath. James died in 1922 as a result of the flu epidemic, May was pregnant with their fourth child at the time.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Puzzle: find the kisses five Togherstown Ballinea Sunday My dearest James, xYou are indeed
a very very good boy to think
of me for Sunday I was very
glad to get letter but I will have
better next Sunday Please God.
We met Mrs Kiernan at mass
she was going that eve to call
on the bride for curiosity will
there be many calling on us
for curiosity I wonder. But
the best news of the day ГўВҖВ”
Johnnie had a letter from
a fellow in Pims saying the
clerkГўВҖВҷs job was certain when
the directors meet on the
29 Feb. It will be grand
if he gets it Mrs S & himself
are gone to Ballymore today
& mother & Christy to Mearscourt
(WynneГўВҖВҷs) I am after being on one of the
corporal works of charity, down
seeing Essy & Paddy Nolan.
They are suffering from colds &
mumps and so I have special sympathy
for them. Mrs Nolan came too.
She read with me so I brought
her in and showed her my ring
fancy that to be the second
time for her to be in the house
She did not adopt any of
JackГўВҖВҷs curiosity she was here
before when Jennie was getting
married, of course there was no
luck she did not wish me.
About the ploughing match ГўВҖВ”
Joe only took third prize 10/ГўВҖВ” It
was better than nothing but he
was very indignant when
the decisions were given he
declared he could plough with
any of them when he came home
he told my mother he took
first & they wouldnГўВҖВҷt give
it to him. Favour in Hell he says,
none of this would be heard only
he got a few bottles of stout
to make him brave he is in a
grate state about pigs he has to sell
afraid anyone else would get
a better price than him for comrade
pigs I am sure you are taking
a great interest in these things
you will be sorry to hear Mrs
SinogThomsom of NugentГўВҖВҷs cross
roads gave a wedding party
for her daughter, all the invited
guests did not turn up so
she is giving another spree
next Sat to give them all
a chance, very kind of herГўВҖВ”
Christy sold his cattle yesterday
ГӮВЈ21ГўВҖВ”10 he got, he was pleased I
think. T Seery got ГӮВЈ21 and he
was saying before his cattle
were better than ours so I donГўВҖВҷt
know which were the best ours
were seen at an advantage
in the summer their father
that might have something to do
with it but sure the poor widow
wanted the 10/ГўВҖВ” worse than Mrs. S.
that was the whole of it I suppose.
Did you know Larry Cunningham one of his sisters, an old girl
of Ned Seery s is going to be x
married to a fellow the name
of Duncan there are a lot
of marriages spoken of; Jocie
Dardis she was at the nursing for
a while is to be married to a
Tom Manley I donГўВҖВҷt think do you
know either party, your mother
might. How did you think of asking
for my ankle you will only
make me pettish like the children
if you take notice of me like that
I seldom feel it now. It must have
only been at started a bit. Ned Seery is going very fast
after Lizzie Fox, they are telling
me but IГўВҖВҷd get it hard to believe
it. They say she meets him etc.
Mike Killian went so far as to say
she is getting fond of him (Ned S).
Tommy is still constant & true. Johnnie
does not seem to have seen anything
to suit in the country. I wonГўВҖВҷt say any more now afraid it
would be taxed so I will wind up with
the usual amount of love from your x loving May Fay
